title: 10 best herbal tinctures for itchy skin

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in pot marigold work together to reduce inflammation, soothe irritated skin, and calm the itching sensation. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infections that can exacerbate itchiness. As a result, using pot marigold tinctures topically or taking it orally can provide rapid relief from itchy skin, making it an effective natural remedy for eczema, psoriasis, and other skin conditions characterized by itching and inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by irritated or inflamed skin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tincture can neutralize free radicals that damage skin cells, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to itchy skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis.
Antipruritic: Pot marigold tincture's antipruritic properties can directly reduce itching by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers itchiness in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties that help soothe itchy skin.

Flowers: The flowers of the pot marigold plant are rich in carotenoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency and fragrance.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low setting.
Combine 100g of dried calendula flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to promote extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which calm irritated skin and reduce itching sensations. The aloe vera gel contains compounds like aloin and barbaloin that have been shown to inhibit histamine release, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating itching. Additionally, the tincture's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from further damage, promoting healthy skin barrier function and relieving discomfort associated with itchy skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation and itching by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.
Antioxidants: Aloe vera's antioxidants, including vitamin C, E, and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can cause skin damage and irritation, reducing itching and discomfort.

Soothing mucilage: Aloe vera's soothing mucilage, a thick, protective gel-like substance, forms a barrier on the skin surface, calming itchiness by moisturizing and protecting the skin from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing itchy skin and reducing redness.
Leaves: Containing natural analgesics and anti-inflammatory agents, they help calm itchy skin, reduce swelling, and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and impurities.
Chop the washed aloe vera leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Combine 200g of chopped aloe vera with 400ml of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar and stir well to mix ingredients.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily to facilitate the tincture-making process.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The herbal extract contains compounds like histamine and serotonin, which work to reduce inflammation and soothe itchy sensations. Additionally, nettle's natural antiseptic and antimicrobial properties help to calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching caused by allergic reactions or environmental factors. By taking a standardized tincture, individuals can experience relief from bothersome itchiness and enjoy smoother, more comfortable skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by skin irritations, such as eczema or acne.
Antihistamine: Nettle's antihistamine properties block the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers itchy reactions in the body, providing relief from itching and allergic reactions.
Sotholistic: Stinging nettle's soothing and moisturizing effects help calm and comfort dry, irritated skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort associated with conditions like atopic dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itchiness.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and itching associated with skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried, using scissors or pinch off individual stems.
Weigh 100g of fresh nettles and combine them with 500ml of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction and maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, labeling it with date and ingredients, and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, work to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation caused by itchiness. Additionally, purple coneflower's antimicrobial properties help to prevent bacterial overgrowth, which can exacerbate itching conditions like eczema and dermatitis. By using an herbal tincture made from purple coneflower, individuals may experience a significant reduction in itchy skin symptoms, leading to improved comfort and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compound, cichoric acid, helps reduce inflammation and itching caused by skin irritations or allergic reactions.
Antimicrobial property: The tincture's antimicrobial compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause skin infections and itchiness.

Soothing and antioxidant properties: The presence of antioxidants like quercetin and kaempferol in purple coneflower tinctures helps to calm and soothe itchy skin, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which help soothe and calm itchy skin.
Flowers: The flowers are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and itching caused by skin irritations, making them a popular choice in herbal remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dark place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24-48 hours.
Grind dried echinacea roots and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder to ensure optimal extraction.
Combine 1 part dried echinacea powder with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, shaking vigorously for 10-15 minutes daily for 2 weeks to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids and storing the resulting tincture in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's mucilage, a thick, gel-like substance, coats the skin, providing a protective barrier that calms irritated skin and reduces itching and inflammation. Additionally, marshmallow root contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy skin cells. As a result, herbal marshmallow tinctures can provide fast and effective relief for itchy skin, making it an excellent natural remedy for various skin conditions.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow's mucilage coats and soothes itchy skin, reducing inflammation and providing a protective barrier against further irritation.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce swelling and itching associated with skin irritations.

Antioxidant activity: Marshmallow's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin inflammation and itchiness, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, a natural soothing agent that helps to calm and relieve itchy skin.
Barks: The barks of the marshmallow plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and irritation associated with itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried althaea officinalis root material for the tincture preparation weighing approximately one ounce.
Grind the harvested dried root material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine the powdered root material with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part herb to four parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean dark glass bottle discarding the solids and storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb contains allantoin, a powerful soothing agent that calms irritated skin, reducing redness and itching. Additionally, comfrey's antifungal and antibacterial compounds help to combat infections that can contribute to itchiness. When applied topically as a tincture, comfrey's natural ingredients work together to provide fast and effective relief from itchy skin, leaving it feeling calm, cool, and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin, a key compound in comfrey tincture, has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations.
Saponins: Comfrey tincture contains saponins, which have emollient and demulcent properties that can help to lock in moisture, soothe dry, itchy skin, and protect the skin from further irritation.

Alkaloids: The alkaloid content in comfrey tincture, particularly symphytine, has antipruritic properties that can directly reduce itching and scratching associated with conditions like eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots are used due to their high concentration of allantoin, a compound that soothes and calms itchy skin.
Buds: Comfrey buds are valued for their rich source of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce itching and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and roots, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants.
Clean and chop the harvested plant material into small pieces, allowing for efficient extraction.
Combine 500g of chopped comfrey material with 750ml of vodka in a glass jar, ensuring proper coverage.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar every other day to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The natural compounds present in the leaves, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and alleviating itching. Additionally, the astringent properties of broad-leaved lime tinctures can help to tighten and shrink swollen skin tissue, providing quick relief from itchy skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to alleviate itchiness and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Broad-leaved lime tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to itchy skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, can help soothe and calm itchy skin by reducing swelling and redness.
Antipruritic properties: Broad-leaved lime tincture contains antipruritic compounds that can directly relieve itching sensations, providing fast and effective relief from itchy skin symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which provide anti-inflammatory properties to soothe itchy skin.
Barks: Contains saponins and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation and itching caused by skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves from mature trees in late spring or early summer.
Rinse the collected leaves with clean water and pat dry with paper towels within an hour.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle for 15-20 minutes.
Combine the chopped leaves with high-proof vodka or ethanol at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks in a dark glass jar.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, then transfer to smaller bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The active compounds in the herb, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching sensations. This can provide relief from conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and bug bites. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to modulate the immune system, which can help to reduce allergic reactions that contribute to itchy skin. By addressing both underlying inflammation and immune response, St John's Wort tinctures offer a natural and effective approach to soothing itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to itchy skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.

Anti-inflammatory action: The bioactive compounds in St John's Wort, including hyperforin and hypericin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations.

Antimicrobial activity: St John's Wort has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat fungal, bacterial, and viral infections that may contribute to itchy skin conditions such as athlete's foot or ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of St John's Wort are used to create tinctures that effectively soothe and calm itchy skin.
Flowers: The bright yellow flowers of St John's Wort contain hyperforin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate itching and discomfort associated with itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves, using scissors or pinch off individual leaves for approximately two ounces total.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces, ensuring uniform size for efficient extraction, taking about five minutes.
Combine one part chopped plant material with one part high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, filling to the top and leaving no air pockets.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar weekly to ensure thorough extraction, requiring patient waiting time.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract, ready for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. The glycyrrhizin in licorice root also exhibits antihistaminic effects, blocking histamine release and alleviating itching sensations. Additionally, licorice has been shown to stimulate the production of cortisol, a hormone that helps regulate skin health and reduce inflammation. By addressing these underlying causes of itchiness, herbal licorice tinctures can provide relief from itchy skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which helps to reduce itching and redness associated with skin irritations.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice tinctures contain flavonoids, such as isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and itchiness.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside present in licorice root, have moisturizing and emollient properties that help to soothe dry, itchy skin, making it an effective natural treatment for skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and calm itchiness by reducing inflammation.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that ease itching and reduce redness associated with skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the morning after dew has dried and dry them thoroughly in shade.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 80% proof vodka or neutral grain spirit at a ratio of 1:4.
Transfer the mixture to clean glass jar and store it in cool dark place for six weeks, shaking daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids, and let it sit for 24 hours.
Filter the resulting tincture one more time using paper coffee filters to ensure clarity and bottle in dark glass bottles for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as taraxasterol and flavonoids, have been shown to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and scratching associated with conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Additionally, dandelion tincture's antimicrobial activity may help combat underlying infections that can exacerbate itchy skin issues. By addressing the root causes of itchiness, dandelion tincture provides natural relief from uncomfortable and distracting skin symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations and allergies.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tincture's antioxidants can neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and skin damage, leading to itchiness and discomfort.
Laxative properties: Dandelion tincture's laxative effects can help relieve constipation, which is often linked to itchy skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming itchy skin.
Leaves: High in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like potassium and calcium, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried dandelion flowers and leaves in amounts of 1 cup per quart.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine with 70% vodka or ethanol.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily, to allow the plant's bioactive compounds dissolve.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 6 months before using, label and date.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for itchy skin are liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and spices that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These tinctures help to calm and relieve itchy skin by reducing inflammation, itching, and scratching, providing instant relief and comfort. For example, oatmeal tincture has been shown to soothe itchy skin caused by eczema, while tea tree oil tincture can help with fungal infections like ringworm. By using these herbal tinctures, people suffering from itchy skin can finally find relief and enjoy a better quality of life, free from the constant discomfort and stress that comes with itchy skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy skin with herbal tinctures is by diluting them in a carrier oil or water and applying topically. Aloe vera, calendula, and chamomile are effective in soothing dry, irritated skin. Mix 1-2 drops of tincture with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil or water and apply it to the affected area 2-3 times daily. Repeat as needed until symptoms subside.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy skin that are treated with herbal tinctures are eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Herbal remedies such as calendula, chamomile, and aloe vera can soothe and calm irritated skin. Tinctures of burdock root, dandelion, and yellow dock can help reduce inflammation and itching associated with these conditions. Additionally, tinctures of lavender and peppermint can provide relief from mild to moderate symptoms of skin irritation and itching.
